steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content:
Rewrite the equation in standard circle form by completing the square for $x$ and $y$.
$$x^2-6x+y^2-10y-30=0$$
$$(x-3)^2-9+(y-5)^2-25-30=0$$
$$(x-3)^2+(y-5)^2=64$$

steps.title: Find the square side
steps.content:
The equation shows that the radius of the circle is $8$. A circle inscribed in a square has a diameter equal to the square side length.
$$2(8)=16$$

steps.title: Find the perimeter
steps.content:
The perimeter of the square is four times the side length.
$$4(16)=64$$
The answer is C.
